metadata.summary: OAuth 2.0 + developer token auth, account hierarchy (MCC → Customer → Campaign → Ad Group), GAQL queries, and credential storage best practices.

content.title: Google Ads API — Basics
content.summary: OAuth 2.0 + developer token auth, account hierarchy (MCC → Customer → Campaign → Ad Group), GAQL queries, and credential storage best practices.

why.p: Google Ads API uses a multi-layer auth model (developer token + OAuth refresh token + optional MCC login_customer_id) that is not obvious from the docs. Getting auth wrong blocks all API calls; getting the account hierarchy wrong produces data in the wrong place.

list.item: Authenticating a Python app to the Google Ads API for the first time
Setting up google-ads.yaml or dict-based credentials
Querying the account hierarchy or listing campaigns with metrics
Updating campaign status (ENABLED, PAUSED, REMOVED)
Search, Display, Shopping, or PMax campaign creation — use the campaign-type-specific methodologies
Conversion tracking setup — use ads-conversion-tracking methodology
Reporting and automation — use google-ads-reporting methodology
Single-account hobby spend under $500/month — Google Ads UI plus auto-rules is cheaper than building API plumbing
One-off creative/copy decisions where human judgement dominates and there is no batching benefit
Smart Bidding tuning during 2-3 week learning phase — let the algorithm finish before agents nudge bids
Anything needing developer-token Standard Access if you only have Test or Basic — apply for upgrade first

content.note: Developer-token Basic Access caps at 15,000 ops/day; bulk migrations or hourly polling will get throttled. API versions deprecate every 9 months (v17 → v18 → v19). Hard-coded version strings break agents on rollover. customer.id is NOT the same as login_customer_id (MCC); confusing them returns USER_PERMISSION_DENIED. Refresh tokens silently expire after 6 months of inactivity or after credential rotation — long-lived agents need re-auth flows. Campaign mutations require update_mask field paths in proto-plus; missing the mask silently drops fields. Cost values are cost_micros (1/1,000,000 of currency) — divide by 1,000,000 before display to humans.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: Run a single sub-agent that loads YAML credentials from a secrets manager, calls a thin wrapper around GoogleAdsClient, and emits structured JSON back to the orchestrator. Wrap every mutation in a dry-run preview that lists the diff (fields, old → new) for human approval before commit. Use GAQL search queries for read paths and batched mutate_* operations (up to 5,000 per request) for write paths. Cache the result of list_accessible_customers() per session so the agent does not re-fetch the MCC tree on every turn. Pin the API version in client config (api_version="v18") and bump deliberately, not implicitly. Use search_stream for any query expected to return over 10k rows; the unary search will OOM. Always SELECT explicit fields — GAQL has no SELECT * and listing fewer fields cuts payload meaningfully. Keep MCC tree discovery out of hot paths — cache list_accessible_customers() result for the agent session. Store developer_token, client_secret, refresh_token in env vars or 1Password / Vault; never the YAML in repo. Tag every mutation with a tracking_url_template parameter or label so re-runs are detectable. For multi-account agents, spawn one client per login_customer_id; mutating across MCCs in one client is error-prone.

section.name: authentication
section.p: OAuth 2.0 + developer token authentication model, credential components, token access levels, and security rules.

list.item: Developer Token: API access identifier from ads.google.com
OAuth 2.0 Client ID: application identifier from Google Cloud Console
OAuth 2.0 Client Secret: application secret from Google Cloud Console
Refresh Token: long-lived token for generating access tokens
Login Customer ID: Manager Account (MCC) ID — required only for MCC access
Test Account: unlimited requests against test data — apply in Google Ads UI
Basic Access: 15,000 daily requests — requires approved application
Standard Access: 10,000 requests per customer — requires company verification

rule.statement: Use a Test Account developer token during development. Promote to Basic or Standard Access only when moving to production with real customer accounts.
rule.rationale: Test Account tokens are approved instantly; Basic and Standard Access require review and verification that blocks development if not planned ahead.

list.item: 1. Create Google Cloud Project
2. Enable Google Ads API in the project
3. Create OAuth 2.0 credentials (web or desktop app)
4. Get Developer Token from ads.google.com account settings
5. Generate Refresh Token via OAuth flow
6. Configure client library with google-ads.yaml or dict

rule.statement: Store all credentials in environment variables or a secrets manager. Never commit credentials to version control.
rule.rationale: Leaked credentials grant full API access to all linked ad accounts, enabling unauthorized spend.

list.item: Use separate credentials for test and production environments
Rotate refresh tokens periodically (at least annually)
Apply least-privilege access — only request scopes needed
LLMs frequently hallucinate cost instead of cost_micros; assert in code that values look right (cost > 1e6 likely already micros)
Models confuse Google Ads API (advertising) with Google Analytics Data API (reporting) — they have separate auth and resources
Agents producing GAQL often forget WHERE segments.date BETWEEN clause and pull lifetime data, blowing the quota
Refresh-token rotation: an agent that fixes auth by minting a new refresh token without revoking the old one can leave dangling credentials
Human-in-loop checkpoint: any mutate_* that touches over 100 resources, changes campaign status to ENABLED, or modifies budget requires explicit confirmation
When parsing enum values from responses, use .name not raw int — agents otherwise emit numeric statuses ("2") to humans

section.name: account-structure
section.p: Resource hierarchy from MCC to keyword, GAQL query patterns for listing customers and campaigns with metrics, and campaign status update pattern.

list.item: Manager Account (MCC) — top level, optional
Customer Account — ad account; parent of all campaigns
Campaign — budget and targeting settings
Ad Group — container for ads and keywords
Ad — creative content
Keyword — search targeting

rule.statement: When querying or mutating resources, always provide the correct customer_id at the level of the resource being accessed, not the MCC ID.
rule.rationale: Using the MCC ID for customer-level operations causes authorization errors. The login_customer_id configures MCC context; customer_id targets the specific account.

section.p: Google Ads uses GAQL (Google Ads Query Language) — a SQL-like query syntax for all report and list operations via GoogleAdsService.search().

example.note: List campaigns with metrics: SELECT campaign.id, campaign.name, campaign.status, metrics.impressions, metrics.clicks, metrics.cost_micros FROM campaign WHERE campaign.status != 'REMOVED' ORDER BY campaign.name. Divide cost_micros by 1,000,000 to get currency value.

antipattern.description: Using the REST API directly instead of the Python client library for complex operations.
antipattern.reason: The client library handles proto-plus marshaling, retry logic, and error code mapping automatically. Raw REST requires manual handling of all edge cases.

rule.statement: Use CampaignOperation with an update_mask to modify only specified fields. Never send a full campaign object for updates.
rule.rationale: Without an update_mask, the API resets all unspecified fields to defaults, corrupting campaign settings.

list.item: Valid status values: ENABLED, PAUSED, REMOVED
REMOVED is permanent for campaigns — use PAUSED to stop delivery temporarily
